description: Improve the `opentabs update` command UX: fetch and display the running server's version, distinguish between background/foreground/dev servers, auto-restart background servers after update, and verify the new version is live.

userStories.title: Fetch and display the running server's version during update
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs update`, I want to see what version the currently running server is on so that I understand the full picture of what's changing.
acceptanceCriteria: When a server is running, the update command displays its version (read from the `x-opentabs-version` response header on `GET /health`)
The version is shown inline with the warning, e.g.: `MCP server v0.0.41 is running on port 9515`
If the `/health` endpoint is unreachable or does not return the header, the warning falls back to the current behavior (no version shown)
userStories.notes: The current `isServerRunning` function in `platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts:44-53` does a simple `fetch` to `/health` and only checks `res.ok`. Change it to also read `res.headers.get('x-opentabs-version')` and return the version string (or null). The unauthenticated `/health` response already includes this header — see `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts:268`. The `isServerRunning` return type should change from `boolean` to an object like `{ running: boolean; version: string | null }` (or similar) so the caller can display the version. Update `handleUpdate` at line 98-103 to use the version in its warning message.

userStories.title: Detect server type: background, foreground, or dev
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs update`, I want the update command to distinguish between a background server (PID file), a foreground server, and a dev proxy so that the post-update instructions are actionable and appropriate.
acceptanceCriteria: The update command detects background servers by checking for a valid PID file at `~/.opentabs/server.pid` and verifying the process is alive with the `x-opentabs-version` header (same logic as `stop.ts`)
The update command detects dev servers by making an authenticated `/health` request and checking for `mode: "dev"` in the response body
Three server types are distinguished: `background` (PID file + alive + verified), `dev` (health response has mode=dev), `foreground` (server responds but no PID file and not dev)
The detection result is used by subsequent stories to tailor the update behavior
userStories.notes: Look at `platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts:48-104` for the PID file + health check verification pattern. The authenticated `/health` response includes a `mode` field (`"dev"` or `"production"`) — see `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts:271-337`. Use `readAuthSecret` from `platform/cli/src/config.ts:74` to get the Bearer token. Import `getPidFilePath` from `platform/cli/src/config.ts` (it re-exports from shared). Define a type like `type ServerType = 'background' | 'foreground' | 'dev'` and a detection function that returns `{ type: ServerType; version: string | null; pid: number | null }`. The PID file check should match stop.ts: read file, parse int, verify alive with `process.kill(pid, 0)`, verify OpenTabs via health check. For dev detection, the auth secret may not exist (first run) — if no secret, we can't distinguish dev from foreground, so default to 'foreground'. This is fine because dev users running from source already get caught by `isRunningFromSource()` at line 69.

userStories.title: Auto-restart background servers after update
userStories.description: As a user who started the server with `opentabs start --background`, I want `opentabs update` to automatically stop and restart the server so that I don't have to manually do it.
acceptanceCriteria: When a background server is detected (PID file + alive + verified), the update command stops it after the npm install completes by sending SIGTERM and waiting up to 5 seconds (same as `opentabs stop`)
After the old server exits, the update command starts a new background server via `opentabs start --background` (spawned as a child process)
After restart, the update command polls `GET /health` for up to 10 seconds to verify the new version is running
On successful restart, prints: `Server restarted. Running v<new_version>.`
If stop fails (process doesn't exit in 5s), prints a warning and tells the user to restart manually — does NOT attempt to start a new server
If start fails (server doesn't respond within 10s), prints a warning suggesting the user check logs
The PID file is cleaned up on stop and re-created by the new start
userStories.notes: This is the core UX improvement. After `performUpdate` succeeds and the server type is `background`, the update command should: (1) SIGTERM the PID, (2) poll for exit (reuse the `waitForExit` pattern from `stop.ts:31-46`), (3) clean up the PID file, (4) spawn `opentabs start --background` as a child process. For spawning the new server, use `spawnSync('opentabs', ['start', '--background', '--port', String(port)], { stdio: 'inherit' })` — this ensures we use the *newly installed* `opentabs` binary from the global path, not the currently-running one. For the health check verification, poll `GET /health` every 500ms for up to 10 seconds, checking the `x-opentabs-version` header matches the expected new version. Important: the port used for start must match the port the old server was on. Pass `--port` explicitly to `opentabs start --background` to preserve the port. The `isProcessAlive` and `waitForExit` helpers should be extracted to a shared module or duplicated (they're small) since they're currently private to stop.ts. Consider extracting them to a utility file like `platform/cli/src/process-utils.ts` that both stop.ts and update.ts can import.

userStories.title: Tailor post-update messages for foreground and dev servers
userStories.description: As a user running a foreground or dev server, I want clear, specific instructions after update so that I know exactly what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: For foreground servers: message says `Server running in foreground on port <port>. Press Ctrl+C in the server terminal, then run: opentabs start`
For dev servers: message says `Dev server detected on port <port>. It will pick up changes on the next rebuild (npm run build).` — no restart instructions
For no server running: no server-related message at all (current behavior preserved)
Messages include the running server version from US-001 (e.g., `Server v0.0.41 running in foreground on port 9515`)
userStories.notes: This story replaces the generic `if (serverRunning)` block at `platform/cli/src/commands/update.ts:120-125` with a switch/if chain on the server type from US-002. For background servers, US-003 already handles the restart — so the background branch prints nothing here (or a brief summary if restart failed). For foreground, the message should be actionable: mention Ctrl+C and the exact command. For dev, the message should explain that `npm run build` in the dev environment triggers the dev-proxy's hot reload, so no manual restart is needed. The dev message is accurate because `dev-proxy.ts` watches `dist/` for changes and auto-restarts the worker. Note: `opentabs update` updates the *globally installed* CLI, not the source tree — so for a dev server, the update is irrelevant to the running dev instance. The message should convey this: the dev server runs from source, not the global install.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for the improved update command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering the new update command behaviors so that future changes don't break the UX improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: server version extraction from `x-opentabs-version` header
Tests cover: server type detection (background with PID file, foreground without PID, dev mode)
Tests cover: the auto-restart flow for background servers (stop + start + verify)
Tests cover: fallback behavior when health check fails
Tests cover: tailored messages for each server type
Test file follows the existing pattern: `platform/cli/src/commands/update.test.ts`
userStories.notes: Look at existing test files for patterns: `platform/cli/src/commands/stop.test.ts` (if it exists) or `platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.test.ts` for how CLI commands are tested. The tests should mock `fetch` (for `/health` responses), `readFile` (for PID file), `spawnSync` (for npm and opentabs commands), `process.kill` (for PID checks), and `fs` operations. Use `vi.mock` for module-level mocks. Test scenarios: (1) background server detected and auto-restarted successfully, (2) background server stop times out, (3) foreground server detected with correct message, (4) dev server detected with correct message, (5) no server running, (6) health check returns version header, (7) health check fails gracefully. If `process-utils.ts` was extracted in US-003, test those helpers directly as well.
